summary: The Goshen Township Zoning Commission held a public hearing on two zoning cases. Case ZC 2025-009, a request to rezone a parcel from B-2 to M-2 heavy industrial for Haywood Utility Contractors' storage yard and garage, was unanimously denied as inconsistent with the State Route 28 corridor plan and Town Center overlay district. Case ZC 2024-006, a final development plan review for expansion of Lakefield Place subdivision (sections 6 and 7), was continued to January 6, 2026 due to unresolved questions about whether a separate out lot must be included in the PUD per Board of Trustees conditions. The commission also approved meeting minutes and the 2026 meeting schedule.
decisions: Approved November 4, 2025 meeting minutes (5-0, one abstention from Pegram)
Approved 2026 Zoning Commission meeting schedule as presented (6-0)
Denied zone change from B-2 to M-2 heavy industrial for ZC 2025-009 per Article 6.02 standards (6-0)
Continued ZC 2024-006 Lakefield Place Final Development Plan review to January 6, 2026 (6-0)
action_items: Zoning Commission members to review previous meeting recordings on Vimeo to determine Board of Trustees stipulations regarding the out lot inclusion in the PUD for Lakefield Place
John (Zoning Director) to work with Haywood family on alternative zoning options or plans that might fit B-2 requirements
Zoning staff to coordinate with Lakefield Place developer (Joe Perugia/Steve from Choice Engineering) before January meeting to clarify out lot status
Haywood applicants encouraged to work with zoning staff and potentially return with a different plan
key_amounts: 40.85 acres - size of Lakefield Place expansion parcel
8,550 sq ft - minimum lot area for Lakefield Place PUD
57 feet - minimum lot width for Lakefield Place PUD
60 by 80 building - proposed shop building size for Haywood Utility Contractors
35% - minimum open space requirement for PDR
30.4% - open space for new sections alone (below 35% minimum without original out lot)
notable_topics: Denial of B-2 to M-2 rezoning request for Haywood Utility Contractors on State Route 28
State Route 28 corridor plan and Town Center overlay district incompatibility with M-2 industrial zoning
Spot zoning concerns for isolated M-2 industrial district
Lakefield Place subdivision expansion final development plan (sections 6 and 7)
Dispute over out lot inclusion in PUD and open space calculations for Lakefield Place
Violation notices issued to Haywood property for unpermitted business operations
attendees: Donna Ross (neighboring property owner, spoke in opposition to Lakefield Place)
notes: Significant portions of the transcript are garbled or contain repeated 'Board of Trustees Meeting, Clermont County, Ohio 45122' placeholder text, likely due to transcription errors. The Haywood property had received violation notices (8-27-25 and 9-26-25) for unpermitted business use and had already obtained a BZA variance for a 20-foot gate. The Clermont County Planning Commission had recommended approval with conditions for the M-2 rezoning, contrary to township staff's denial recommendation. The Lakefield Place case involves a developer (Joe Perugia) who does not want the out lot included in the PUD but Board of Trustees conditions from November 2024 apparently required it. The property has already been rezoned to PUD through the preliminary development plan process.
title: ZC 12-2-25
